idea: add __launch_bounds__(256,1) to reduce register pressure assumptions
justification: The baseline reached only 42.1 TFLOPS on a tiny-M shape, and the nearby small-M shape 256x256x8192 saw a dramatic jump after adding launch bounds; residency pressure is the first grounded structural lever to test.

idea: promote both RHS tensor maps to L2_128B
justification: With N=4096 and K=256, each CTA reuses a narrow RHS slice; nearby small-shape winners improved by selectively promoting RHS maps into L2.

idea: tighten the two stage warpgroup waits from 1 to 0
justification: Small-K kernels have little pipeline depth to hide, so reducing consumer wait distance is a grounded latency experiment once occupancy-focused launch bounds are in place.

idea: promote the output tensor map to L2_128B
justification: The shape writes many small output tiles across 16 N-tiles, so output-side L2 promotion is worth isolating after stabilizing the producer-consumer schedule.

idea: promote all tensor maps to L2_256B
justification: The 256x256x8192 neighbor peaked with all tensor maps promoted to L2_256B; this tests whether the same cache residency trick helps the wider-N but tiny-K shape.
